name: Asparagus Sauce - Great Chefs
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the asparagus and snap off the white ends. Set aside all tops and half that number of ends.
2. Plunge the tops into a large pot of salted, boiling water and cook until limp but not for too long. Cook slightly longer than for eating; if undercooked, they will not process well; if overcooked, they will taste earthy.
3. Drain the asparagus thoroughly while still hot, then run it through a food processor with unsalted butter for 3-4 minutes until smooth, scraping down the sides of the processor frequently.
4. Simultaneously, chop the asparagus ends to remove dry hard field cuts and simmer with cream. Cook the ends slowly for 20 minutes, then strain gently.
5. Just before serving, combine the asparagus, butter, and cream. Heat slowly to just below boiling point.
6. Adjust seasonings to taste.
Preparation time: 3/4 Hour
